items.title: Top Nifty50 stocks analysts suggest buying in this volatile week
items.description: Stock Reports Plus, powered by Refinitiv, is a comprehensive research report that evaluates five key components of 4,000+ listed stocks – earnings, fundamentals, relative valuation, risk, and price momentum – to generate standardised scores. The simple average of the above-mentioned five component ratings is normally distributed to reach an average score.

items.title: F&O profits at risk: How much one lot of Nifty futures and options costs after the STT hike
items.description: The Union Budget 2026 has increased Securities Transaction Tax (STT) on equity derivatives, impacting futures and options trading costs. STT on Nifty futures has risen from 0.02% to 0.05%, increasing the cost per lot by approximately Rs 500. Options trading also sees a 50% STT hike on premiums, raising concerns about market liquidity and foreign participation.

items.title: Netweb Technologies among 4 stocks showing bullish RSI upswing
items.description: On February 1, four Nifty500 stocks that gained over 3% were highlighted in the RSI Trending Up scan, based on data from StockEdge.com. It is an uptrend signal if the RSI crosses above 50 from below. "RSI trending up" means that the Relative Strength Index (RSI) values are increasing, suggesting strengthening momentum in the price of the stock. This can indicate potential upward movement and may be used by traders to identify buying opportunities.

items.title: Sensex jumps over 300 pts, Nifty above 24,850 as investors assess annual budget
items.description: Sensex and Nifty rebounded from losses in a special weekend session, as investors assessed the implications of the federal budget, which fell short of expectations for measures to attract foreign capital.

items.title: Hindustan Copper shares jump over 6%, snap 2-day fall despite sharp decline in Copper prices
items.description: Hindustan Copper shares rebounded on Monday despite a sharp drop in global copper prices, as value buyers stepped in after a steep two-day correction. The stock had fallen 21% across Friday and Sunday’s special Budget session, with metal price weakness continuing to weigh on near-term sentim

items.title: ITC, Godfrey Phillips and other tobacco shares fall up to 5% as cigarette prices rise after excise duty hike
items.description: ITC, Godfrey Phillips and VST Industries fell sharply on Monday after cigarette prices jumped following a steep excise duty hike effective February 1. Retail prices rose by Rs 22–25 per pack as taxes increased sharply, raising concerns over demand pressure, margin impact and a potential rise in illicit cigarette trade.

items.title: MCX shares crack 4%, down 20% in 3 days as gold, silver rout deepens for third consecutive day
items.description: MCX shares tumble 20% in 3 days as gold and silver prices experience a significant decline, impacting volume and earnings. This volatility stems from speculation surrounding US monetary policy and the Federal Reserve chair nomination. Despite external pressures, MCX reported a substantial 151% year-on-year jump in net profit for Q3FY26, driven by robust operational performance and increased trading activity.

items.title: Hero MotoCorp shares in focus after 26% jump in January dispatches
items.description: Hero MotoCorp shares are expected to stay in focus after the company reported strong year-on-year growth in January dispatches, supported by healthy scooter and motorcycle demand. Rising EV traction through VIDA and steady international performance reinforced its leadership position, even as near-term technical indicators reflected mild pressure.

items.title: Hindustan Zinc shares rise 4% despite silver’s Rs 1.44 lakh crash in 3 days. Is this a buying opportunity?
items.description: Hindustan Zinc shares rose over 4% even as Silver prices posted a significant drop. This comes after Hindustan Zinc recently became India's most valued metal company. Analysts have provided positive ratings and target prices for the stock. The company reported strong Q3 earnings and has plans for future capital expenditure to support expansion.

items.title: Stocks to buy in 2026 for long term: Paytm, Canara Bank among 5 stocks that could give 10-30% return
items.description: Brokerage firms have rolled out a fresh set of stock calls across sectors, pointing to selective upside opportunities in the current market.We have collated a list of recommendations from top brokerage firms from ETNow and other sources:

items.title: Negative Breakout: These 13 stocks cross below their 200 DMAs
items.description: In the Nifty500 pack, the closing prices of 29 stocks fell below their 200-day moving averages on February 01, according to StockEdge.com's technical scan data. Of these, we have highlighted 13 stocks that slipped more than 4%. Trading below the 200 DMA is considered a negative signal because it indicates that the stock's price is below its long-term trend line. The 200 DMA is used as a key indicator by traders for determining the overall trend in a particular stock. Take a look:

items.title: Q3 results today: Hyundai India among 72 companies to report earnings on Monday
items.description: Indian markets brace for a busy Monday as 72 companies, including Hyundai Motor India and Bajaj Housing Finance, unveil their December quarter earnings. Analysts anticipate robust revenue growth for Hyundai, driven by a strong product mix. Bajaj Housing Finance is expected to show steady AUM growth, though NIM compression is a watchpoint. Investors await these key results.

items.title: GIFT Nifty up 90 points; here's the trading setup for today's session
items.description: The Union Budget for FY27 maintained capex growth at 12% despite revenue challenges, with short-term borrowings rising. The Nifty saw a 2% correction on Budget Day, with analysts predicting limited further downside. A rupee recovery and an early India-US trade deal conclusion are key for a market rebound.

items.title: Stocks in news: BEL, HAL, ITC, Hero MotoCorp, Anant Raj, Adani Green
items.description: Indian markets saw a sharp fall on Budget Day. Investors reacted to a tax hike on futures and options. While the Budget offers long-term growth, near-term volatility is expected. Several companies like BEL, HAL, and Hero MotoCorp are in focus due to budget announcements and financial results. Adani Green also issued a clarification.

items.title: Asian shares drop as gold plunge deepens, dollar gains
items.description: Global markets experienced a downturn on Monday. Stocks and precious metals saw significant losses. The Japanese yen weakened considerably. This sentiment follows a volatile week and precedes crucial central bank decisions and economic reports. Investors are adjusting expectations amid these developments.

items.title: Sharp hike in STT pulls Nifty and Sensex down by 2%; brokerages feel the heat
items.description: India's recent hike in Securities Transaction Tax (STT) on futures and options has triggered a significant market downturn, mirroring a similar event in 2004. The move, aimed at curbing speculation, has increased trading costs for all participants, impacting brokers and foreign investors particularly hard.

items.title: Budget 2026 not headline-grabbing but fiscally prudent and growth-oriented
items.description: The Union Budget 2026-27 delivers fiscal prudence without sacrificing growth, keeping deficit consolidation on track while sustaining strong capex. Markets reacted cautiously to the STT hike, but measures on buybacks, PIS liberalisation, bond market development and support for sunrise sectors signal a steady, reform-oriented roadmap focused on long-term competitiveness.

items.title: Buy, Sell or Hold: Citi maintains sell on Jindal Steel; Bernstein maintains outperform on Bajaj Auto
items.description: Brokerage calls show a mixed picture for investors. Citi remains cautious on Jindal Steel and Power, citing weaker profitability and pressure on realizations, while Bernstein turns bullish on Bajaj Auto, backed by premiumisation, improving margins, and export recovery. Citi sees continued auto volume momentum in January but expects limited Budget impact on overall consumption, maintaining Maruti Suzuki, M&M, and Hyundai as top picks.

items.title: Anant Raj, Netweb Tech, other data centre stocks rally up to 7%. What’s triggering the surge?
items.description: Indian data centre stocks rallied as the Union Budget announced a tax holiday until 2047 for foreign cloud service providers, aiming to position India as a global hub. Companies like Anant Raj, Techno Electric, and Netweb Technologies are expected to benefit from increased data centre development and infrastructure demand.

items.title: Groww shares slide nearly 5% after Budget raises STT on F&O trades
items.description: Billionbrains Garage Ventures, Groww's parent, saw its shares tumble following the Union Budget's hike in Securities Transaction Tax on futures and options. The increased STT rates, impacting derivatives trading, led to a broad sell-off in brokerage and exchange stocks. While potentially equity-positive long-term, the move could cause short-term portfolio rebalancing.

items.title: Budget 2026 ticks all boxes, reasonable opportunity to invest in next 3-4 months: Prashant Jain
items.description: Prashant Jain said Union Budget 2026–27 ticks all the right boxes and offers a reasonable investment opportunity over the next three to four months despite market volatility. He believes large-cap stocks are fairly valued, long-term returns should track nominal GDP growth, and policy stability and fiscal discipline support sustained economic expansion.

items.title: Latent View Analytics shares rally 8% after Q3FY26 profit up 19% YoY
items.description: Latent View Analytics shares jumped nearly 8% after the company posted a 19% year-on-year rise in Q3FY26 profit and strong revenue growth. Improved margins, new client additions and rising institutional participation supported sentiment, while management reiterated focus on scaling AI and Agentic AI capabilities.

items.title: Budget crash? Don't panic. Analysts spot 35 structural stock picks to ride out the storm
items.description: Despite a sharp post-Budget market sell-off, brokerages remain bullish on structurally strong themes ranging from defence and infrastructure to data centres, EVs and rare earths. With capital expenditure rising and policy continuity intact, analysts have identified 35 stocks positioned to benefit from India’s long-term growth strategy.

items.title: Defence stocks face test of nerves as 22% capex lift in Union Budget meets lofty valuations; BEL, HAL, Mazagon Dock top picks
items.description: India’s defence stocks enter a volatile phase despite a 22% jump in Budget capex, as valuations run ahead of fundamentals. Brokerages remain bullish on BEL, HAL and Mazagon Dock, citing strong order pipelines and indigenisation tailwinds, but warn that execution risks and lofty expectations could cap near-term upside.

items.title: Budget stock picks: SBI Securities select 5 stocks with up to 17% upside. Do you own any?
items.description: Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman doubled down on the government’s infrastructure led growth strategy in Union Budget 2026, proposing a sharp increase in capital expenditure to Rs 12.2 lakh crore for FY2026-27, up from Rs 11.2 lakh crore in the current fiscal. From a market perspective, the capex heavy Budget announcements have brought several stocks into focus, and SBI Securities has selected five names that could benefit from the announcement. Here is the full list.

items.title: Gold, silver, Sensex, Nifty all slide: Where should investors park money now?
items.description: Gold, silver and equities tumbled together in a rare cross-asset slide, with leveraged unwinding, a stronger dollar and expectations around Kevin Warsh’s Fed outlook triggering a sharp selloff. Analysts warn against chasing rallies, suggesting disciplined asset allocation as precious metals consolidate and large-cap equities offer relatively steadier opportunities.

items.title: Stock picks of the week: 5 stocks with consistent score improvement and upside potential of up to 30%
items.description: For those worried about what will happen to the stock markets, history has some comforting lessons. Markets have the ability to tackle (and price in) many uncertainties. And volatility is part of every bull run. Just because a majority of stocks have not performed in the last one year does not mean that the long-term story has changed. Yes, one has to be a bit cautious while selecting stocks. Our selected stocks for today depict a strong upward trajectory in their overall average score which is based on five key pillars: Earnings, fundamentals, relative valuation, risk, and price momentum. This implies that there has been a significant improvement in their market outlook in the given time frame.
